_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for Kataglyphis brand styling. Every colour and font used by the CV, the book, the presentation and the docs website is defined here exactly once -- do not hardcode any of these values anywhere else. A value of the form '@other_key' is an alias, so a literal is never written twice; `colors_dark` may alias into `colors`. Derived files (LaTeX snippets, CSS tokens, Pandoc YAML) are generated by style/generate_style.py -- run it with --write after editing; CI enforces --check. Other applications should read the generated style/brand.tokens.json, which is this file with all aliases already resolved.
name: Kataglyphis
_syntax_comment: Code-highlighting palette, shared by the PDFs (via the generated Pandoc .theme files) and the website (via the generated Pygments styles), so a code block looks the same in the book and on the docs site. `syntax` is the light/print palette, `syntax_dark` the dark one.

fonts._mono_comment: Latin Modern Mono matches the metrics LaTeX uses by default, so the book keeps its tuned line breaking. Scale=1 defeats Pandoc's Scale=MatchLowercase (which would widen it against Roboto), and the explicit faces supply the bold/italic that the 'Latin Modern Mono' family itself lacks -- its bold ships in the 'Light' family. mono_fallback covers glyphs Latin Modern Mono does not have (the slides use λ and ∑); without it LuaTeX drops them from the PDF silently.
